stripe_search_customers1

stripe_list_charges1
crm_list_engagements1

mortgage_los_update_document1

crm_create_engagement1
Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. If the deployed LOS requires an authenticated staff context before reads, use mortgage_los_login once with email set to sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com, then use mortgage_los_search_loans with query set to a single string that includes LN-2026-00621 and DeShawn Choi terms to obtain the internal loan_id, loan status, closing_date, title_company on record, and borrower linkage needed for all later LOS calls.
2. Use mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id with loan_id from step 1 together with mortgage_los_get_documents with the same loan_id (and optional status filter on documents if used) to capture the current checklist rows including hoi_binder and title-related documents as stored in LOS. If the agent already has the public loan number only, mortgage_los_get_loan with loan_number LN-2026-00621 may be used to retrieve the same loan record including the internal loan_id before calling mortgage_los_get_documents. Do not use mortgage_los_get_outstanding_documents as a stand-in for this single loan checklist because that tool lists outstanding items across loans by assignment filters, not by a single loan_id.
3. Use mortgage_los_get_activity_log with the same loan_id to see whether a dated LOS trail already exists or the log is empty before adding a new activity.
4. Use search_emails with the required query parameter set to a string that includes LN-2026-00621, DeShawn Choi, deshawn.choi@icloud.com, Keisha, HOI binder, Tryon, b.hensley@tryontitle.com, cash to close, and Friday, repeating calls with folder_name INBOX and other folders such as SENT if needed, to list candidate email_id values and snippets for borrower-to-Sofia questions, borrower-to-Keisha binder threads, and any Tryon-signed mail for this file if present.
5. Use get_email_by_id with each email_id from step 4 together with the correct folder_name when the tool requires it to read full bodies, attachment fields, recipients, and cc paths. When a returned row exposes a thread identifier distinct from the email_id, use get_thread with that thread_id to load the whole thread; if no thread_id is available, stay on get_email_by_id per message.
6. Use channels_list with a channel_types value allowed by the tool so standard workspace channels are returned, then match display names to closings and loan-processing to capture their channel_id values. Then pull closings traffic using conversations_history with channel_id set to the closings id and/or conversations_search_messages with search_query containing LN-2026-00621, Choi, and HOI and filter_in_channel set to that closings channel id, so Tyler Washington appears when the tool returns that display name on messages.
7. When closings traffic from step 6 shows an expandable thread, use conversations_replies with channel_id set to the closings id and thread_ts set to the thread root timestamp: prefer the non-empty thread_ts on child messages, otherwise use the parent message ts as thread_ts when Slack shows replies under a parent whose own thread_ts is empty, then confirm whether any replies supplied sourced file or checklist evidence or whether questions stayed unanswered; skip this call when messages are standalone with no replies.
8. Use conversations_history with channel_id set to the loan-processing id from step 6 and/or conversations_search_messages with search_query containing LN-2026-00621, DeShawn, Fri or Friday, cash to close, HOI, and last week and filter_in_channel set to the loan-processing channel id to collect parallel speculation that the binder arrived but was never attached to the file.
9. When loan-processing traffic from step 8 shows an expandable thread, use conversations_replies with channel_id set to the loan-processing id and thread_ts resolved the same way as in step 7 so threaded follow-ups the flat history missed are loaded; skip when there is no thread.
10. Use crm_search_contacts with full_name set to DeShawn Choi or close variants the tool accepts to obtain the CRM contact id string returned in search results, optionally followed by crm_get_contact with id set to that same string if full contact fields are needed, then use crm_list_engagements with contact_ids set to an array containing that id so call and note bodies load for Friday closing, cash to close, missing HOI, personal inbox routing, and pre-close review notes.
11. Use contacts_search_contacts with queries Tryon Title, Barbara Hensley, and b.hensley@tryontitle.com and use contacts_get_contact with any returned contact_id as needed to load the internal title contact record and compare it to signer lines on any Tryon emails from steps 4 and 5.
12. Use stripe_search_customers with query set to a string that includes Choi, DeShawn, or deshawn.choi@icloud.com to locate the Stripe customer id, then stripe_list_charges with customer_id set to that id (and optional stripe_search_charges with query set to LN-2026-00621 if listing alone is ambiguous) to determine whether any charge ties to LN-2026-00621 versus charges that only reference other Choi loan numbers.
13. If after steps 1 through 12 a genuine investor or title follow-up is still required, use contacts_search_contacts to resolve recipient addresses, then send_email with sender set to the active processor mailbox such as sofia.reyes@keystonemortgage.com, recipients and cc filled from discovered contacts, plus subject and content with the concrete ask implied by the prompt.
14. Use mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id from step 1, action set to the note-style action label the LOS accepts for narrative entries, detail set to the full chronology and reconciliation conclusion across LOS, email, both Slack channels, CRM, and Stripe, and author optionally set to the acting staff identity if the tool is used that way in the environment.
15. Use conversations_add_message with channel_id set to the closings channel id from step 6 and payload set to a single post that summarizes the reconciled HOI binder story, cites where evidence lives, and points readers to the new LOS activity entry from step 14.
16. If a new CRM note would only stack noise on top of existing engagements, skip CRM writes entirely; if one concise engagement can supersede the contradictory older CRM narrative without spamming duplicates, use crm_create_engagement with engagement_type set to note or the closest supported type, body set to the reconciled facts that retire the old contradiction, contact_ids set to an array containing the DeShawn Choi CRM id from step 10, and title optional but short if used.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 A loan activity entry was added (via mortgage_los_add_activity) using loan_id los_loan_dc34bc01927c (the internal LOS id for LN-2026-00621 in the base universe), and the tool response indicates success.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The second mortgage_los_add_activity call used loan_id 'los_loan_dc34bc01927c' with action 'note'. Tool result (tool_call_result_24.json) confirms success with id '2c243705-deff-440b-8264-d7053aabc461' and loan_id 'los_loan_dc34bc01927c'.
✓Run #2The second mortgage_los_add_activity call used loan_id 'los_loan_dc34bc01927c' with action 'note', and tool_call_result_22.json confirms success with a returned id, loan_id, timestamp, author, and full detail field.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows mortgage_los_add_activity was called with loan_id 'los_loan_dc34bc01927c' and action 'note'. The tool response (tool_call_result_22.json) confirms success with the activity record showing id, loan_id=los_loan_dc34bc01927c, and the full detail field.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows mortgage_los_add_activity called with loan_id 'los_loan_dc34bc01927c'. The first attempt failed (invalid action 'compliance_review'), but the second attempt with action 'compliance_alert' succeeded, returning a record with loan_id 'los_loan_dc34bc01927c' and a success result (tool_call_result_19.json).
✓Run #5The agent's first mortgage_los_add_activity call failed (invalid action value). The second call used action='note' with loan_id='los_loan_dc34bc01927c' and the tool result (tool_call_result_14.json) confirms success with id, timestamps, and the full detail text returned.
✓Run #6The second mortgage_los_add_activity call (after the first failed with an invalid action enum) used loan_id 'los_loan_dc34bc01927c' with action 'note', and tool_call_result_20.json confirms success with the same loan_id returned in the response.
2 A message was posted (via conversations_add_message) to Slack channel C003 (closings in the base universe), and the tool response indicates success.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1conversations_add_message was called with channel_id 'C003'. Tool result (tool_call_result_22.json) shows ok:true and a successful message timestamp, confirming the post succeeded.
✓Run #2The conversations_add_message call used channel_id '#closings'. The channels_list confirmed C003=closings, and the tool response (tool_call_result_21.json) shows ok:true with the message text about LN-2026-00621 HOI binder, confirming successful posting to the closings channel.
✓Run #3conversations_add_message was called with channel_id 'C003' and the result (tool_call_result_23.json) shows ok:true with a message timestamp, confirming success.
✓Run #4conversations_add_message was called with channel_id 'C003' and the response (tool_call_result_20.json) shows ok: true, confirming success.
✓Run #5The agent called conversations_add_message with channel_id='#closings'. The channels_list call confirmed C003 maps to 'closings'. The tool returned ok:true with the posted message content. The criterion allows the closings channel id from channels_list; the system accepted '#closings' and the post succeeded.
✓Run #6conversations_add_message was called with channel_id 'C003' and tool_call_result_19.json shows ok:true in the response, confirming successful posting.
3 The detail (or equivalent narrative field) passed to mortgage_los_add_activity mentions the homeowners insurance binder or HOI and references both the email routing issue (such as Keisha, personal inbox, borrower sent to wrong address, misrouted, or similar) and internal channel or team confusion (such as closings, loan-processing, Tyler, missing from file, not in checklist, or similar wording).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The LOS activity detail explicitly mentions 'HOI binder', 'Keisha's PERSONAL Gmail (keisha.williams@gmail.com)' (email routing issue), and '#closings' and 'Tyler ran pre-close, posted twice in #closings asking for HOI — no replies in thread' (internal channel confusion). Both required narrative threads are present.
✓Run #2The detail field in the successful mortgage_los_add_activity call explicitly mentions 'HOI', references the email routing issue (Keisha's personal Gmail keisha.williams@gmail.com), and also references internal channel confusion (Tyler in #closings, Keisha in #loan-processing, contradictory CRM notes).
✓Run #3The mortgage_los_add_activity detail explicitly mentions 'HOI BINDER RECONCILIATION', 'keisha.williams@gmail.com (personal inbox)', email routing (borrower sent to personal vs company email, both unread), and internal channel confusion ('Tyler Washington flagged in #closings', 'Keisha posted in #loan-processing'). Both required narrative threads are present.
✓Run #4The mortgage_los_add_activity detail explicitly mentions HOI binder reconciliation, references Keisha's personal gmail (email routing root cause), and references Tyler's pre-close review postings in #closings with zero sourced answers (internal channel confusion), satisfying both required narrative threads.
✓Run #5The detail in the successful mortgage_los_add_activity explicitly covers the HOI binder, states the borrower 'sent HOI binder to Keisha Williams — first to personal gmail (wrong address), then to company email,' and references '#loan-processing she thought it came in but couldn't find it in the file' and 'Tyler Washington asked in #closings for the binder — zero replies.' Both required narrative threads are present.
✓Run #6The mortgage_los_add_activity detail explicitly mentions 'HOI binder exists in Keisha's work inbox — never forwarded into file', 'Borrower emailed HOI binder to keisha.williams@gmail.com (personal email, wrong inbox)', 'Tyler Washington posted in #closings twice asking for HOI binder during pre-close', and '#loan-processing' activity, satisfying both the email routing and internal channel confusion requirements.
4 The payload passed to conversations_add_message for closings mentions LN-2026-00621 or 00621 together with HOI, homeowners insurance, or binder (or similar), and also points the team to where the cleanup chronology lives on the loan (for example LOS activity, loan activity log, loan note, LOS feed, or phrasing like logged or posted on the loan) created for this cleanup.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The closings message payload explicitly references 'LN-2026-00621', 'HOI binder', and states 'I've logged the full chronology on the loan activity.' — a clear pointer to the loan-side record.
✓Run #2The closings message payload contains 'LN-2026-00621', 'HOI binder', and points to the loan-side record: 'I've posted a summary in #closings and logged a note on the loan'. The final response also states 'LOS activity log — Dated note with full chronology and findings logged on the loan'.
✓Run #3The closings channel message explicitly mentions 'LN-2026-00621', 'HOI binder', and 'Activity log updated on the loan' — pointing the team to where the chronology lives on the loan.
✓Run #4The conversations_add_message payload mentions 'LN-2026-00621', 'HOI binder', and explicitly directs readers: 'I've logged the full chronology as a compliance_alert on the loan activity feed' — an unambiguous pointer to the loan-side record.
✓Run #5The closings message explicitly mentions 'LN-2026-00621' and 'HOI binder' and states 'Activity logged on the loan,' pointing the team to the loan-side chronology record that was created.
✓Run #6The closings message payload mentions 'LN-2026-00621 (DeShawn Choi)', 'HOI binder', and explicitly says 'check the loan activity log — it's all in one place now', pointing the team to the loan-side chronology record.
5 The final response to the user states that the LOS document checklist shows hoi_binder or homeowners insurance binder as received (or received in March 2026, or similar) while internal channels or CRM still reflected confusion, missing binder, or misrouted mail (or similar contradiction).
outcome
 ✗✓✓✗✓✓ 67%
✗Run #1The final response is very brief: 'All tasks complete. The paper trail is reconciled, the team has one answer instead of three, and the chase is in motion.' It does not state what the LOS checklist shows (received status, March 2026) nor the contradiction with internal channels/CRM confusion. The required reconciled story is absent from the final user-facing response.
